<h1 id="understand-and-explore-windows-settings">Understand and explore Windows Settings</h1>


<div class="learnArticleContent">
	<p>This article is for people who use a screen reader program such as Windows Narrator, JAWS, or NVDA with Windows tools or features and Microsoft 365 products. This article is part of the <a href="/en-us/topic/cf945192-600d-431a-b8b8-c7b7c7fd90e6" data-linktype="absolute-path">Accessibility help & learning</a> content set where you can find more accessibility information on our apps. For general help, visit <a href="/en-us/" data-linktype="absolute-path">Microsoft Support</a>.</p>
<div class="NOTE">
<p>Note</p>
<p>To learn more about screen readers, go to <a href="../office-accessibility/how-screen-readers-work-with-microsoft-365" data-linktype="relative-path">How screen readers work with Microsoft 365</a>.</p>
</div>
<div class="tabGroup" id="tabgroup_1">
<ul role="tablist">
<li role="presentation">
<a href="#tabpanel_1_windows-11" role="tab" aria-controls="tabpanel_1_windows-11" data-tab="windows-11" tabindex="0" aria-selected="true" data-linktype="self-bookmark">Windows 11</a>
</li>
<li role="presentation">
<a href="#tabpanel_1_windows-10" role="tab" aria-controls="tabpanel_1_windows-10" data-tab="windows-10" tabindex="-1" data-linktype="self-bookmark">Windows 10</a>
</li>
</ul>
<section id="tabpanel_1_windows-11" role="tabpanel" data-tab="windows-11">

<p>The Settings app in Windows 11 offers a centralized hub to manage and customize device preferences. This includes categories such as System, Bluetooth & Devices, Network & Internet, Personalization, Apps, Accounts, Time & Language, Accessibility, Privacy & Security, Gaming, and Windows Update. This guide provides a detailed description of the setting categories in the Settings app. </p>
<h2 id="in-this-topic">In this topic</h2>
<ul>
<li><p><a href="#openwindows_11" data-linktype="self-bookmark">Open Windows Settings</a></p>
</li>
<li><p><a href="#navigatethrough_11" data-linktype="self-bookmark">Navigate through settings categories</a></p>
<ul>
<li><a href="#navigatesystemsett_11" data-linktype="self-bookmark">Navigate System settings</a></li>
<li><a href="#navigateblue_11" data-linktype="self-bookmark">Navigate Bluetooth and devices settings</a></li>
<li><a href="#navigatenetwork_11" data-linktype="self-bookmark">Navigate Network and Internet settings</a></li>
<li><a href="#navigateperson_11" data-linktype="self-bookmark">Navigate Personalization settings</a></li>
<li><a href="#navigateapps_11" data-linktype="self-bookmark">Navigate Apps settings</a></li>
<li><a href="#navigateaccount_11" data-linktype="self-bookmark">Navigate Accounts settings</a></li>
<li><a href="#navigatetime_11" data-linktype="self-bookmark">Navigate Time and language settings</a></li>
<li><a href="#navigategaming_11" data-linktype="self-bookmark">Navigate Gaming settings</a></li>
<li><a href="#navigateaccessib_11" data-linktype="self-bookmark">Navigate Accessibility settings</a></li>
<li><a href="#navigateprivacy_11" data-linktype="self-bookmark">Navigate Privacy and security settings</a></li>
<li><a href="#navigatewindows_11" data-linktype="self-bookmark">Navigate Windows Update settings</a></li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p><a id="openwindows_11"></a></p>
<h2 id="open-windows-settings">Open Windows Settings</h2>
<p>Press <strong>Windows</strong> <img src="../media/windowslogo.png" role="presentation" data-linktype="relative-path">
 key <strong>+I</strong> to open the Windows settings window. Your screen reader reads, "Settings window, search box; find a setting."</p>
<p>For more information, go to <a href="use-a-screen-reader-to-explore-and-navigate-settings-in-windows" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader to explore and navigate Settings in Windows.</a></p>
<p><a id="navigatethrough_11"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-through-settings-categories">Navigate through settings categories</h2>
<ol>
<li><p>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to the <strong>System</strong> settings.</p>
</li>
<li><p>To navigate between categories, press the <strong>Up</strong> or <strong>Down arrow</strong> key and then press <strong>Enter</strong> to open it.</p>
</li>
<li><p>Use <strong>Tab</strong> and <strong>arrow</strong> keys to move through the options within the selected section and press <strong>Backspace</strong> to return to the Settings window.</p>
<div class="NOTE">
<p>Note</p>
<p>To search for a specific setting, press <strong>Ctrl+E</strong>. The focus moves to the search box of the Settings window. Use the <strong>arrow</strong> keys to browse the search results and press <strong>Enter</strong> to open the selected setting.</p>
</div>
</li>
</ol>
<p><a id="navigatesystemsett_11"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-system-settings">Navigate System settings</h2>
<p>The <strong>System</strong> section in the Settings app allows you to manage essential features such as display, sound, notifications, storage, and power settings ensuring your device runs smoothly and efficiently.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate System settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app, then press <strong>Tab</strong> key until you hear "System," and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Up</strong> or <strong>Down</strong> arrow key to explore options for managing display, sound, notifications, power, and storage settings.</li>
</ol>
<div class="NOTE">
<p>Note</p>
<ul>
<li>Surface device users can use the Surface app to customize their interaction with the device. The available settings and customization options depend on the specific type of Surface device being used.</li>
<li>If you don't have the Surface app installed, you can <a href="https://www.microsoft.com/store/productId/9WZDNCRFJB8P" data-linktype="external">download the Surface app from the Microsoft Store.</a> When it has downloaded, select <strong>Start</strong>, search for <strong>Surface</strong>, then select the app from the list of results.</li>
</ul>
</div>
<p>To explore some commonly used system settings such as display, clipboard, notifications, screensharing and projecting, and power management, check out the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/3f67a2f2-5c65-ceca-778b-5858fc007041" data-linktype="absolute-path">Change display brightness and color in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/ddcbbcd4-0a02-f6e4-fe14-6766d850f294" data-linktype="absolute-path">Change notification and quick settings in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/30375039-ce71-9fe4-5b30-21b7aab6b13f" data-linktype="absolute-path">Using the clipboard</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/5af9f371-c704-1c7f-8f0d-fa607551d09c" data-linktype="absolute-path">Screen mirroring and projecting to your PC or wireless display</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/0efbfe40-e3e2-581b-13f4-1a0e9936c2d9" data-linktype="absolute-path">Share things with nearby devices in Windows</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigateblue_11"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-bluetooth-and-devices-settings">Navigate Bluetooth and devices settings</h2>
<p>The Bluetooth and devices section lets you manage devices connected to your computer, including wireless peripherals such as mouse, keyboards, speakers, headphones, and more.</p>
<p>Additionally, this section includes the Phone Link feature under the Mobile devices page, which allows you to connect your Android or iPhone to your PC.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Bluetooth and devices settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to the <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Press <strong>Down arrow</strong> key until you hear "Bluetooth and devices," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use <strong>Up</strong> or <strong>Down arrow</strong> key to explore available options for connecting, removing, or managing devices.</li>
</ol>
<div class="NOTE">
<p>Note</p>
<ul>
<li>Surface device users can use the Surface app to customize their interaction with the device. The available settings and customization options depend on the specific type of Surface device being used.</li>
<li>If you don't have the Surface app installed, you can <a href="https://www.microsoft.com/store/productId/9WZDNCRFJB8P" data-linktype="external">download the Surface app from the Microsoft Store.</a> When it has downloaded, select <strong>Start</strong>, search for <strong>Surface</strong>, then select the app from the list of results.</li>
</ul>
</div>
<p>To explore some commonly used Bluetooth and devices settings, such as mouse, keyboard, hearing devices, and phone link, refer to the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="make-your-mouse-keyboard-and-other-input-devices-easier-to-use" data-linktype="relative-path">Make your mouse, keyboard, and other input devices easier to use</a></li>
<li><a href="use-a-screen-reader-to-pair-a-bluetooth-device-in-windows" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader to pair a Bluetooth device in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="using-hearing-devices-with-your-windows-11-pc" data-linktype="relative-path">Using hearing devices with your Windows 11 PC</a></li>
<li><a href="../phone-link/use-a-screen-reader-to-navigate-phone-link-app" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader to navigate Phone Link</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigatenetwork_11"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-network-and-internet-settings">Navigate Network and Internet settings</h2>
<p>The Network and Internet section allows you to manage your device's connectivity, including Wi-Fi, Ethernet, mobile hotspots, and airplane mode settings.</p>
<p>Additionally, this section includes settings for dial-Up connections, which allow you to configure and edit dial-up connections directly within the Settings app. Improvements have been made to dial-up settings to allow for making changes and managing the connections inside of the Settings app.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Network and Internet settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Press <strong>Down arrow</strong> key until you hear "Network and Internet," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Up</strong> or <strong>Down arrow</strong> key to explore options for connecting to networks, troubleshooting connectivity issues, or managing data usage.</li>
</ol>
<p>For information on how to connect to a Wi-Fi network, refer to <a href="/en-us/topic/1f881677-b569-0cd5-010d-e3cd3579d263" data-linktype="absolute-path">Connect to a Wi-Fi network in Windows</a> And <a href="/en-us/topic/97914e31-3aa4-406d-cef6-f1629e2c3721" data-linktype="absolute-path">Setting up a wireless network in Windows.</a></p>
<p><a id="navigateperson_11"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-personalization-settings">Navigate Personalization settings</h2>
<p>The Personalization section allows you to customize the appearance of your device, including themes, backgrounds, colors, lock screen, taskbar settings, and more. These features enable you to create a unique and visually appealing desktop experience tailored to your preferences.</p>
<p>You can also manage Text Input settings to personalize your on-screen keyboard and typing insights, optimizing the typing experience to suit your needs. The Fonts section lets you view installed fonts or add new ones, allowing further customization of the text style across your device.</p>
<p>Additionally, personalization settings include features such as Interactive Weather on Lock Screen and Desktop Spotlight, offering options to enhance your lock screen with real-time weather updates or rotating images and details. If you want to adjust how you use your device to get personalized tips, ads, and recommendations within Microsoft experience, navigate the Device Usage settings in the Settings app for further customization options.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Personalization settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Press <strong>Down arrow</strong> key until you hear "Personalization," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Up</strong> or <strong>Down arrow</strong> key to explore options for updating your device's look and feel to suit your preferences.</li>
</ol>
<p>To explore some commonly used personalization settings such as lock screen, dynamic lighting, refer to the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/81dab9b0-35cf-887c-84a0-6de8ef72bea0" data-linktype="absolute-path">Personalize your lock screen</a></li>
<li><a href="make-windows-easier-to-see" data-linktype="relative-path">Make Windows easier to see</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/347661a5-7d7a-4b6d-951a-287612461f40" data-linktype="absolute-path">Personalization based on your device usage settings and your privacy</a></li>
<li><a href="use-a-screen-reader-to-explore-and-navigate-the-windows-desktop" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader to explore and navigate Windows Desktop</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/8e8f22e3-e820-476c-8f9d-9ffc7b6ffcd2" data-linktype="absolute-path">Control your Dynamic Lighting devices in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/3f67a2f2-5c65-ceca-778b-5858fc007041" data-linktype="absolute-path">Change display brightness and color in Windows</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigateapps_11"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-apps-settings">Navigate Apps settings</h2>
<p>The Apps section allows you to manage the programs installed on your device. This includes uninstalling and managing apps and setting default programs. This section provides access to configuring offline maps, which let you download maps for use without an internet connection, and apps for websites, where you can control which apps open web links.</p>
<p>Additionally, the Startup settings enable you to manage which apps launch automatically when your device starts, helping to optimize performance.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Apps settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Press <strong>Down arrow</strong> key until you hear "Apps," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Up</strong> or <strong>Down arrow</strong> key to explore options for managing your installed apps, advanced app settings, and startup behavior.</li>
</ol>
<p>To explore some commonly used app settings such as uninstall apps, startup configuration, and offline maps, refer to the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/4b55f974-2cc6-2d2b-d092-5905080eaf98" data-linktype="absolute-path">Uninstall or remove apps and programs in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/115a420a-0bff-4a6f-90e0-1934c844e473" data-linktype="absolute-path">Configure startup applications in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/51ece9fb-a0f2-9853-4164-6940865085c8" data-linktype="absolute-path">Find your way with Maps</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigateaccount_11"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-account-settings">Navigate Account settings</h2>
<p>The Accounts section allows you to manage user accounts, sign-in options, and account preferences. You can choose to sign in with a personal, work, or school account, ensuring seamless access to resources and settings across devices.</p>
<p>This section provides options to enhance security by customizing sign-in methods such as Windows Hello (facial recognition, fingerprint, or PIN), traditional passwords, and security keys. Additionally, you can configure email and account settings for connected apps, enabling the synchronization of emails, calendars, and contacts.</p>
<p>You can also use the Windows Backup settings within this section to back up your important files, preferences, and settings to the cloud, ensuring you can restore them easily on a new or existing device.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Accounts settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Press <strong>Down arrow</strong> key until you hear "Accounts," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Up</strong> or <strong>Down arrow</strong> key to explore options related to account.</li>
</ol>
<p>To explore some commonly used account settings such as sign in options, backup and restore data, refer to the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="use-a-screen-reader-to-back-up-and-restore-data-on-windows" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader to back up and restore data on Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/dae28983-8242-bb2a-d3d1-87c9d265a5f0" data-linktype="absolute-path">Configure Windows Hello</a></li>
<li><a href="use-windows-hello-with-a-screen-reader" data-linktype="relative-path">Use Windows Hello with a screen reader</a></li>
<li><a href="use-a-screen-reader-to-navigate-sign-in-options-in-windows" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader to navigate sign in options in Windows</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigatetime_11"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-time-and-language-settings">Navigate Time and language settings</h2>
<p>The Time and language section includes settings for date and time, language and region, typing, and speech (your device's language and voice options). You can adjust the time zone, enable automatic updates for time settings, configure preferred display languages, and manage input and speech preferences.</p>
<p>Additionally, the typing settings allow you to personalize your typing experience with features such as autocorrect, text suggestions, and multilingual input options. The Windows typing and speech features enhance your typing efficiency by providing insights such as spelling and grammar corrections, predictive text suggestions, and more, improving your overall productivity.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Time and Language settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Press <strong>Down arrow</strong> key until you hear "Time and Language," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Up</strong> or <strong>Down arrow</strong> key to explore options for managing time settings, language preferences, and speech configurations.</li>
</ol>
<p>To explore some commonly used time and language settings such as date and time, language and region, typing, and speech, refer to the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/dfaa7122-479f-5b98-2a7b-fa0b6e01b261" data-linktype="absolute-path">How to set your time and time zone</a></li>
<li><a href="enable-text-suggestions-in-windows" data-linktype="relative-path">Enable text suggestions in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/12a10cb4-8626-9b77-0ccb-5013e0c7c7a2" data-linktype="absolute-path">Manage the language and keyboard/input layout settings in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/83ff75bd-63eb-0b6c-18d4-6fae94050571" data-linktype="absolute-path">Use voice recognition in Windows</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigategaming_11"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-gaming-settings">Navigate Gaming settings</h2>
<p>The Gaming section allows you to customize settings for an enhanced gaming experience, including configuring the Game bar for capturing and sharing gameplay, managing Captures for recording and saving your location, and enabling Game mode to optimize system performance during gaming sessions.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Gaming settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Press <strong>Down arrow</strong> key until you hear "Gaming," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Up</strong> or <strong>Down arrow</strong> key to explore options for customizing the Game Bar, adjusting capture settings, and enabling Game Mode.</li>
</ol>
<p>To explore some commonly used gaming settings such as game mode and game bar, refer to the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="https://support.xbox.com/en-IN/help/games-apps/game-setup-and-play/use-game-mode-gaming-on-pc" data-linktype="external">Use Game Mode while gaming on your Windows device</a></li>
<li><a href="https://support.xbox.com/help/games-apps/game-setup-and-play/customize-game-bar-on-windows-10" data-linktype="external">Customize Game Bar on Windows devices</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigateaccessib_11"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-accessibility-settings">Navigate Accessibility settings</h2>
<p>The Accessibility section provides a range of settings designed to make Windows easier to use. The system organizes these settings into three main sections:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Vision:</strong> This section includes options to adjust text size, enable visual effects, customize the mouse pointer and touch indicator, configure the text cursor, use the Magnifier, apply color filters, activate contrast themes, and use the Narrator screen reader.</li>
<li><strong>Hearing:</strong> This section offers settings for managing audio related options, configuring hearing devices, and enabling captions.</li>
<li><strong>Interaction:</strong> This section allows customization of speech, keyboard, mouse, and eye control for more efficient device interaction.</li>
</ol>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Accessibility settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Press <strong>Down arrow</strong> key until you hear "Accessibility," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Up</strong> or <strong>Down arrow</strong> key to explore the available settings for vision, hearing, and interaction features.</li>
</ol>
<p>For more information about Accessibility settings such as text size, color filters, text cursor, hearing devices, speech, keyboard, and mouse, refer to the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="make-it-easier-to-focus-on-tasks" data-linktype="relative-path">Make it easier to focus on tasks</a></li>
<li><a href="make-windows-easier-to-hear" data-linktype="relative-path">Make Windows easier to hear</a></li>
<li><a href="make-windows-easier-to-see" data-linktype="relative-path">Make Windows easier to see</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/1d5830c3-eee3-8eaa-836b-abcc37d99b9a" data-linktype="absolute-path">Change the size of text in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="voice-access/set-up-voice-access" data-linktype="relative-path">Set up voice access</a></li>
<li><a href="make-your-mouse-keyboard-and-other-input-devices-easier-to-use" data-linktype="relative-path">Make your mouse, keyboard, and other input devices easier to use</a></li>
<li><a href="use-a-screen-reader-and-keyboard-to-navigate-the-windows-accessibility-flyout" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader and keyboard to navigate the Windows Accessibility Flyout</a></li>
<li><a href="eye-control/get-started-with-eye-control-in-windows" data-linktype="relative-path">Get started with eye control in Windows</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigateprivacy_11"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-privacy-and-security-settings">Navigate Privacy and security settings</h2>
<p>The Privacy and security section allows you to manage your device's data privacy and security features. This includes settings for app permissions, where you can control what information apps can access, and Windows security, which helps protect your device with tools such as antivirus, firewall, and device encryption.</p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Press <strong>Down arrow</strong> key until you hear "Privacy and security," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Up</strong> or <strong>Down arrow</strong> key to explore options for managing privacy preferences and enhancing your device's security.</li>
</ol>
<div class="NOTE">
<p>Note</p>
<ul>
<li>Surface device users can use the Surface app to customize their interaction with the device. The available settings and customization options depend on the specific type of Surface device being used.</li>
<li>If you don't have the Surface app installed, you can <a href="https://www.microsoft.com/store/productId/9WZDNCRFJB8P" data-linktype="external">download the Surface app from the Microsoft Store.</a> When it has downloaded, select <strong>Start</strong>, search for <strong>Surface</strong>, then select the app from the list of results.</li>
</ul>
</div>
<p>To know more about Privacy and security settings, refer to following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="use-a-screen-reader-and-keyboard-to-navigate-privacy-settings-in-windows" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader and keyboard to navigate privacy settings in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/7c7f6a09-cebd-5589-c376-7f505e5bf65a" data-linktype="absolute-path">General privacy settings in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="https://www.microsoft.com/privacy/privacystatement#mainwindowsmodule" data-linktype="external">Microsoft Privacy Statement</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigatewindows_11"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-windows-update-settings">Navigate Windows Update settings</h2>
<p>The Windows Update section allows you to manage updates for your device, ensuring it stays secure and runs efficiently. You can check for updates, adjust update preferences, and manage advanced options, such as update pauses and delivery optimization.</p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Press <strong>Down arrow</strong> key until you hear "Windows Update," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Up</strong> or <strong>Down arrow</strong> keys to explore options for managing update settings and scheduling restarts.</li>
</ol>
<p>For more information about Windows Update settings such as install Windows update, refer to the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="use-a-screen-reader-to-check-and-run-windows-updates" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader to check and run Windows updates</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/3c5ae7fc-9fb6-9af1-1984-b5e0412c556a" data-linktype="absolute-path">Install Windows Updates</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/bf86a244-8f26-a3c7-a137-a43bfbe688e8" data-linktype="absolute-path">Windows Update Delivery Optimization and privacy</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/ef20bb3d-40f4-20cc-ba3c-a72c844b563c" data-linktype="absolute-path">Join the Windows Insider Program and manage Insider settings</a></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="tabpanel_1_windows-10" role="tabpanel" data-tab="windows-10" aria-hidden="true" hidden="hidden">

<p>The Settings app in Windows 10 provides a central hub to manage and customize device preferences. This covers categories such as System, Devices, Network & Internet, Personalization, Apps, Accounts, Time & Language, Gaming, Ease of Access, Search, Privacy, and Update & Security. This guide provides a detailed description of the setting categories in the Settings app and exploring them with a screen reader.</p>

</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<p><a id="windows_10"></a></p>
<h2 id="open-windows-settings-1">Open Windows Settings</h2>
<p>Press <strong>Windows</strong> <img src="../media/windowslogo.png" role="presentation" data-linktype="relative-path">
 key <strong>+I</strong> to open the Windows settings window. Your screen reader reads "Settings window, search box, find a setting."</p>
<p>For more information, go to <a href="use-a-screen-reader-to-explore-and-navigate-settings-in-windows" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader to explore and navigate Settings in Windows.</a></p>
<p><a id="navigatetgrough_10"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-through-settings-categories-1">Navigate through settings categories</h2>
<ol>
<li><p>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</p>
</li>
<li><p>To navigate between categories, press the <strong>Up</strong> or <strong>Down arrow</strong> key and then press <strong>Enter</strong> to open it.</p>
</li>
<li><p>Use <strong>Tab</strong> and <strong>arrow</strong> keys to move through the options within the selected section and press <strong>Backspace</strong> to return to the <strong>Settings</strong> window.</p>
<div class="NOTE">
<p>Note</p>
<p>To search for a specific setting, press <strong>Ctrl+E</strong>. The focus moves to the search box of the Settings window. Use the <strong>arrow</strong> keys to browse the search results and press <strong>Enter</strong> to open the selected setting.</p>
</div>
</li>
</ol>
<p><a id="navigatesystem_10"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-system-settings-1">Navigate System settings</h2>
<p>The <strong>System</strong> section in the Settings app allows you to manage essential features such as display, sound, notifications, storage, and power settings, ensuring your device runs smoothly and efficiently.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate System settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app, then press <strong>Tab</strong> key until you hear "System," and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>arrow</strong> keys to explore options for managing display, sound, notifications, power, and storage settings.</li>
</ol>
<p>To explore some commonly used system settings such as display, clipboard, notifications, screensharing and projecting, and power management, check out the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/3f67a2f2-5c65-ceca-778b-5858fc007041" data-linktype="absolute-path">Change display brightness and color in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/ddcbbcd4-0a02-f6e4-fe14-6766d850f294" data-linktype="absolute-path">Change notification and quick settings in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/30375039-ce71-9fe4-5b30-21b7aab6b13f" data-linktype="absolute-path">Using the clipboard</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/5af9f371-c704-1c7f-8f0d-fa607551d09c" data-linktype="absolute-path">Screen mirroring and projecting to your PC or wireless display</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/0efbfe40-e3e2-581b-13f4-1a0e9936c2d9" data-linktype="absolute-path">Share things with nearby devices in Windows</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigatedevice_10"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-devices-settings">Navigate Devices settings</h2>
<p>The Bluetooth and devices section lets you manage devices connected to your computer, including wireless peripherals such as mouse, keyboards, speakers, headphones, and more.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Bluetooth and devices settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Navigate using <strong>arrow</strong> keys until you hear "Devices," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Tab</strong> and <strong>arrow</strong> keys to explore available options for connecting, removing, or managing devices.</li>
</ol>
<p>To explore some commonly used device settings, such as mouse and keyboard, refer to the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="make-your-mouse-keyboard-and-other-input-devices-easier-to-use" data-linktype="relative-path">Make your mouse, keyboard, and other input devices easier to use</a></li>
<li><a href="use-a-screen-reader-to-pair-a-bluetooth-device-in-windows" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader to pair a Bluetooth device in Windows</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigatemobile_10"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-mobile-devices-settings">Navigate Mobile devices settings</h2>
<p>The Mobile devices section allows you to manage settings for connected mobile devices, such as smartphones and tablets. This includes configuring features such as mobile hotspot, syncing, and tethering options to integrate your mobile device with your PC.</p>
<p>Additionally, this section includes the Phone Link feature, which allows you to connect your Android or iPhone to your PC.</p>
<p>For more information, refer to <a href="../phone-link/use-a-screen-reader-to-navigate-phone-link-app" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader to navigate Phone Link</a>.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Mobile Devices settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to the System settings.</li>
<li>Navigate using <strong>arrow</strong> keys until you hear "Mobile devices," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Tab</strong> and <strong>arrow</strong> keys to explore available options for managing mobile device connections and settings.</li>
</ol>
<p><a id="navigatenetwork_10"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-network-and-internet-settings-1">Navigate Network and Internet settings</h2>
<p>The Network and Internet section allows you to manage your device's connectivity, including Wi-Fi, Ethernet, mobile hotspots, and airplane mode settings.</p>
<p>Additionally, this section includes settings for dial-Up connections, which allow you to configure and edit dial-up connections directly within the Settings app. Improvements have been made to dial-up settings to allow for making changes and managing the connections inside of the Settings app.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Network and Internet settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Navigate using <strong>arrow</strong> keys until you hear "Network and Internet," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Tab</strong> and <strong>arrow</strong> keys to explore options for connecting to networks, troubleshooting connectivity issues, or managing data usage.</li>
</ol>
<p>For information on how to connect to a Wi-Fi network, refer to Connect to a <a href="/en-us/topic/1f881677-b569-0cd5-010d-e3cd3579d263" data-linktype="absolute-path">Wi-Fi network in Windows</a>. And <a href="/en-us/topic/97914e31-3aa4-406d-cef6-f1629e2c3721" data-linktype="absolute-path">Setting up a wireless network in Windows.</a></p>
<p><a id="navigatetperson_10"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-personalization-settings-1">Navigate Personalization settings</h2>
<p>The Personalization section allows you to customize the appearance of your device, including themes, backgrounds, colors, lock screen, taskbar settings, and more. These features enable you to create a unique and visually appealing desktop experience tailored to your preferences.</p>
<p>You can also manage Text Input settings to personalize your on-screen keyboard and typing insights, optimizing the typing experience to suit your needs. The Fonts section lets you view installed fonts or add new ones, allowing further customization of the text style across your device.</p>
<p>Additionally, personalization settings include features such as Interactive Weather on Lock Screen and Desktop Spotlight, offering options to enhance your lock screen with real-time weather updates or rotating images and details. If you want to adjust how you use your device to get personalized tips, ads, and recommendations within Microsoft experience, navigate the Device Usage settings in the Settings app for further customization options.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Personalization settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Navigate using <strong>arrow</strong> keys until you hear "Personalization," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Tab</strong> and <strong>arrow</strong> keys to explore options for updating your device's look and feel to suit your preferences.</li>
</ol>
<p>To explore some commonly used personalization settings such as lock screen, refer to the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/81dab9b0-35cf-887c-84a0-6de8ef72bea0" data-linktype="absolute-path">Personalize your lock screen</a></li>
<li><a href="make-windows-easier-to-see" data-linktype="relative-path">Make Windows easier to see</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/347661a5-7d7a-4b6d-951a-287612461f40" data-linktype="absolute-path">Personalization based on your device usage settings and your privacy</a></li>
<li><a href="use-a-screen-reader-to-explore-and-navigate-the-windows-desktop" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader to explore and navigate Windows Desktop</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/8e8f22e3-e820-476c-8f9d-9ffc7b6ffcd2" data-linktype="absolute-path">Control your Dynamic Lighting devices in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/3f67a2f2-5c65-ceca-778b-5858fc007041" data-linktype="absolute-path">Change display brightness and color in Windows</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigatetapps_10"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-apps-settings-1">Navigate Apps settings</h2>
<p>The Apps section allows you to manage the programs installed on your device. This includes uninstalling and managing apps and setting default programs. This section provides access to configuring Offline Maps, which let you download maps for use without an internet connection, and Apps for Websites, where you can control which apps open web links.</p>
<p>Additionally, the Startup settings enable you to manage which apps launch automatically when your device starts, helping to optimize performance.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Apps settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Navigate using <strong>arrow</strong> keys until you hear "Apps," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Tab</strong> and <strong>arrow</strong> keys to explore options for managing your installed apps, advanced app settings, and startup behavior.</li>
</ol>
<p>To explore some commonly used apps settings such as Uninstall apps, startup configuration, and offline maps, refer to the following articles, refer to the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/4b55f974-2cc6-2d2b-d092-5905080eaf98" data-linktype="absolute-path">Uninstall or remove apps and programs in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/115a420a-0bff-4a6f-90e0-1934c844e473" data-linktype="absolute-path">Configure startup applications in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/51ece9fb-a0f2-9853-4164-6940865085c8" data-linktype="absolute-path">Find your way with Maps</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigateaccount_10"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-account-settings-1">Navigate Account settings</h2>
<p>The Accounts section allows you to manage user accounts, sign-in options, and account preferences. You can choose to sign in with a personal, work, or school account, ensuring seamless access to resources and settings across devices.</p>
<p>This section provides options to enhance security by customizing sign-in methods such as Windows Hello (facial recognition, fingerprint, or PIN), traditional passwords, and security keys. Additionally, you can configure email and account settings for connected apps, enabling the synchronization of emails, calendars, and contacts.</p>
<p>You can also use the Windows Backup settings within this section to back up your important files, preferences, and settings to the cloud, ensuring you can restore them easily on a new or existing device.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Accounts settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li><strong>Navigate</strong> using <strong>arrow</strong> keys until you hear "Accounts," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Tab</strong> and <strong>arrow</strong> keys to explore options related to account.</li>
</ol>
<p>To explore some commonly used account settings such as sign in options, backup and restore data, refer to the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="use-a-screen-reader-to-back-up-and-restore-data-on-windows" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader to back up and restore data on Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/dae28983-8242-bb2a-d3d1-87c9d265a5f0" data-linktype="absolute-path">Configure Windows Hello</a></li>
<li><a href="use-windows-hello-with-a-screen-reader" data-linktype="relative-path">Use Windows Hello with a screen reader</a></li>
<li><a href="use-a-screen-reader-to-navigate-sign-in-options-in-windows" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader to navigate sign in options in Windows</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigatetime_10"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-time-and-language-settings-1">Navigate Time and language settings</h2>
<p>The Time and language section includes settings for date and time, language and region, typing, and speech (your device's language and voice options). You can adjust the time zone, enable automatic updates for time settings, configure preferred display languages, and manage input and speech preferences.</p>
<p>Additionally, the typing settings allow you to personalize your typing experience with features such as autocorrect, text suggestions, and multilingual input options. The Windows typing and speech features enhance your typing efficiency by providing insights such as spelling and grammar corrections, predictive text suggestions, and more, improving your overall productivity.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Time and language settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Navigate using <strong>arrow</strong> keys until you hear "Time and language," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Tab</strong> and <strong>arrow</strong> keys to explore options for managing time settings, language preferences, and speech configurations.</li>
</ol>
<p>To explore some commonly used Time and language settings such as date and time, language and region, typing, and speech, refer to the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/dfaa7122-479f-5b98-2a7b-fa0b6e01b261" data-linktype="absolute-path">How to set your time and time zone</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/12a10cb4-8626-9b77-0ccb-5013e0c7c7a2" data-linktype="absolute-path">Manage the language and keyboard/input layout settings in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/83ff75bd-63eb-0b6c-18d4-6fae94050571" data-linktype="absolute-path">Use voice recognition in Windows</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigategaming_10"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-gaming-settings-1">Navigate Gaming settings</h2>
<p>The Gaming section allows you to customize settings for an enhanced gaming experience, including configuring the Game Bar for capturing and sharing gameplay, managing Captures for recording and saving your location, and enabling Game Mode to optimize system performance during gaming sessions.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Gaming settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Navigate using <strong>arrow</strong> keys until you hear "Gaming," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Tab</strong> and <strong>arrow</strong> keys to explore options for customizing the Game Bar, adjusting capture settings, and enabling Game Mode.</li>
</ol>
<p>To explore some commonly used gaming settings, such as game mode and game bar, refer to the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="https://support.xbox.com/en-IN/help/games-apps/game-setup-and-play/use-game-mode-gaming-on-pc" data-linktype="external">Use Game Mode while gaming on your Windows device</a></li>
<li><a href="https://support.xbox.com/help/games-apps/game-setup-and-play/customize-game-bar-on-windows-10" data-linktype="external">Customize Game Bar on Windows devices</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigateease_10"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-ease-of-access-settings">Navigate Ease of Access settings</h2>
<p>The Ease of Access section provides a range of settings designed to make Windows easier to use. The system organizes these settings into three main sections:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Vision:</strong> This section includes options to adjust text size, enable visual effects, customize the mouse pointer and touch indicator, configure the text cursor, use the Magnifier, apply color filters, activate contrast themes, and use the Narrator screen reader.</li>
<li><strong>Hearing:</strong> This section offers settings for managing audio related options, configuring hearing devices, and enabling captions.</li>
<li><strong>Interaction:</strong> This section allows customization of speech, keyboard, mouse, and eye control for more efficient device interaction.</li>
</ol>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Ease of Access settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Navigate using <strong>arrow</strong> keys until you hear "Ease of Access," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Tab</strong> and <strong>arrow</strong> keys to explore the available settings for vision, hearing, and interaction features.</li>
</ol>
<p>For more information about Ease of Access settings such as text size, color filters, text cursor, hearing devices, speech, keyboard, and mouse, refer to the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="make-it-easier-to-focus-on-tasks" data-linktype="relative-path">Make it easier to focus on tasks</a></li>
<li><a href="make-windows-easier-to-hear" data-linktype="relative-path">Make Windows easier to hear</a></li>
<li><a href="make-windows-easier-to-see" data-linktype="relative-path">Make Windows easier to see</a></li>
<li><a href="change-the-size-of-text-in-windows" data-linktype="relative-path">Change the size of text in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="make-your-mouse-keyboard-and-other-input-devices-easier-to-use" data-linktype="relative-path">Make your mouse, keyboard, and other input devices easier to use</a></li>
<li><a href="use-a-screen-reader-and-keyboard-to-navigate-the-windows-accessibility-flyout" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader and keyboard to navigate the Windows Accessibility Flyout</a></li>
<li><a href="eye-control/get-started-with-eye-control-in-windows" data-linktype="relative-path">Get started with eye control in Windows</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigatesearch_10"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-search-settings">Navigate Search settings</h2>
<p>The Search section allows you to customize how Windows searches for files and information on your device. You can configure search locations by managing Find My Files, control the storage and use of data for searches by managing permissions and history.</p>
<p><strong>Steps to navigate Search settings</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to System settings.</li>
<li>Navigate using <strong>arrow</strong> keys until you hear "Search," then press Enter.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Tab</strong> and <strong>arrow</strong> keys to explore options for managing file search preferences, permissions, and history.</li>
</ol>
<p><a id="navigateprivacy_10"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-privacy-settings">Navigate Privacy settings</h2>
<p>The Privacy section allows you to manage your device's data privacy and security features. This includes settings for App Permissions, where you can control what information apps can access.</p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Navigate using <strong>arrow</strong> keys until you hear "Privacy," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Tab</strong> and <strong>arrow</strong> keys to explore options for managing privacy preferences and enhancing your device's security.</li>
</ol>
<p>To know more about Privacy settings, refer to following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="use-a-screen-reader-and-keyboard-to-navigate-privacy-settings-in-windows" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader and keyboard to navigate privacy settings in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/7c7f6a09-cebd-5589-c376-7f505e5bf65a" data-linktype="absolute-path">General privacy settings in Windows</a></li>
<li><a href="https://www.microsoft.com/privacy/privacystatement#mainwindowsmodule" data-linktype="external">Microsoft Privacy Statement</a></li>
</ul>
<p><a id="navigatetupdate_10"></a></p>
<h2 id="navigate-update-and-security-settings">Navigate Update and Security settings</h2>
<p>The Update and Security section allows you to manage updates for your device, ensuring it stays secure and runs efficiently. You can check for updates, adjust update preferences, and use Windows security, which helps protect your device with tools such as antivirus, firewall, and device security.</p>
<ol>
<li>Open the Settings app and use <strong>Tab</strong> key to move the focus to <strong>System</strong> settings.</li>
<li>Navigate using <strong>arrow</strong> keys until you hear "Update and security," then press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li>
<li>Use the <strong>Tab</strong> and <strong>arrow</strong> keys to explore options for managing update settings and scheduling restarts.</li>
</ol>
<p>For more information about Update and security settings such as install Windows update, refer to the following articles:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="use-a-screen-reader-to-check-and-run-windows-updates" data-linktype="relative-path">Use a screen reader to check and run Windows updates</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/3c5ae7fc-9fb6-9af1-1984-b5e0412c556a" data-linktype="absolute-path">Install Windows Updates</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/cc3cb85b-91d7-7e56-8ce1-db50b4d18d0b" data-linktype="absolute-path">Use a fix-it tool with Windows 10</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/bf86a244-8f26-a3c7-a137-a43bfbe688e8" data-linktype="absolute-path">Windows Update Delivery Optimization and privacy</a></li>
<li><a href="/en-us/topic/ef20bb3d-40f4-20cc-ba3c-a72c844b563c" data-linktype="absolute-path">Join the Windows Insider Program and manage Insider settings</a></li>
</ul>
</section>

<h2 id="technical-support-for-customers-with-disabilities">Technical support for customers with disabilities</h2>
<p>Microsoft wants to provide the best possible experience for all our customers. If you have a disability or questions related to accessibility, please contact the <a href="https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/p/?LinkID=518252" data-linktype="external">Microsoft Disability Answer Desk</a> for technical assistance. The Disability Answer Desk support team is trained in using many popular assistive technologies and can offer assistance in English, Spanish, French, and American Sign Language. Please go to the Microsoft Disability Answer Desk site to find out the contact details for your region.</p>
<p>If you are a government, commercial, or enterprise user, please contact <a href="https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkId=824629" data-linktype="external">the enterprise Disability Answer Desk</a>.</p>

<script id="uhf-footer-ccpa">
    const globalPrivacyControlEnabled = navigator.globalPrivacyControl;

    const GPC_DataSharingOptIn = (globalPrivacyControlEnabled) ? false : checkThirdPartyAdsOptOutCookie();

    if(window.onGPCLoaded) {
        window.onGPCLoaded();
    }
    
    function checkThirdPartyAdsOptOutCookie() {
        try {
            const ThirdPartyAdsOptOutCookieName = '3PAdsOptOut';
            var cookieValue = getCookie(ThirdPartyAdsOptOutCookieName);
            return cookieValue != 1;
        } catch {
            return true;
        }
    }

    function getCookie(cookieName) {
        var cookieValue = document.cookie.match('(^|;)\\s*' + cookieName + '\\s*=\\s*([^;]+)');
        return (cookieValue) ? cookieValue[2] : '';
    }
</script>

<script type="text/javascript">
	(function () {
		function getCookie(name) {
			var cookies = document.cookie.split('; ');
			for (var i = 0; i < cookies.length; i++) {
				var kvp = cookies[i].split('=');
				if (kvp[0] === name) {
					return decodeURIComponent(kvp[1]);
				}
			}
			return null;
		}

		if (typeof window.oneDS === 'object') {
			window.analytics = new window.oneDS.ApplicationInsights();
			var config = {
				instrumentationKey: "ac04587c8d6b439297eee84d88c95e8b-e1b6c3bf-fa11-485c-aebc-a825e1f68c8f-7432",
				disableCookiesUsage: true,
				cookieCfg: {
					ignoreCookies: ["MicrosoftApplicationsTelemetryDeviceId", "MSFPC"]
				},
				channelConfiguration: {
					eventsLimitInMem: 50
				},
				propertyConfiguration: {
					gpcDataSharingOptIn: false,
					callback: {
						userConsentDetails: () => {
							const consentDetails = {
								Required: true,
								Analytics: false,
								Advertising: false,
								SocialMedia: false
							};

							return consentDetails;
						}
					}
				},
				webAnalyticsConfiguration: {
					autoCapture: {
						pageView: true,
						onLoad: true,
						onUnload: true,
						click: true,
						scroll: true,
						resize: true,
						lineage: false,
						jsError: true,
						msTags: true
					},
					autoPopulateParentIdAndParentName: true,
					isLoggedIn: false,
					urlCollectHash: true,
urlCollectQuery: true,
					useDefaultContentName: true,
					useShortNameForContentBlob: false
				}
			};

			var isFirstTime = true;
			var jsLocalId = null;


			//Telemetry initializers are used to modify the contents of collected telemetry before being sent from the user's browser
			window.analytics.addTelemetryInitializer(function (e) {
				if (isFirstTime) {
					var context = window.analytics.getPropertyManager().getPropertiesContext();
					var ext = e.ext = e.ext || {};

					if (jsLocalId) {
						// Set the localId so it's set automatically for all future events
						context.user.setLocalId(jsLocalId);

						var userExt = ext.user = ext.user || {};
						userExt.localId = jsLocalId;
					}

					isFirstTime = false;
				}
			});
			window.analytics.initialize(config, []);
		}
	})();
</script>

		

<script type="text/javascript">
	$(function () {
		var signInUrl = "/en-us/authentication/signin";
		var signOutUrl = "/en-us/authentication/signout";
		var switchAccountUrl = "/en-us/authentication/switchaccount";
		var shellOptions = {
			meControlOptions: {
				apiGeneration: 'GEN2',
				authProviderConfig: {
					type: 'webAadWithMsaProxy',
					appSignInUrl: signInUrl,
					appSignInToUrl: signInUrl,
					appSignOutUrl: signOutUrl,
					appSwitchUrl: switchAccountUrl,
					appSwitchToUrl: switchAccountUrl,
					aad: {
						signOutUrl: 'https://login.microsoftonline.com:443/uxlogout?appid=ee272b19-4411-433f-8f28-5c13cb6fd407\u0026wreply=https%3A%2F%2Fsupport.microsoft.com%2Fsignin-oidc\u0026shouldforgetuser=False',
						signOutAndForgetUrl: 'https://login.microsoftonline.com:443/uxlogout?appid=ee272b19-4411-433f-8f28-5c13cb6fd407\u0026wreply=https%3A%2F%2Fsupport.microsoft.com%2Fsignin-oidc\u0026shouldforgetuser=True',
						forgetUrl: 'https://login.microsoftonline.com:443/forgetuser',
						rememberedAccountsUrl: 'https://login.microsoftonline.com:443/savedusers?appid=ee272b19-4411-433f-8f28-5c13cb6fd407\u0026wreply=https%3A%2F%2Fsupport.microsoft.com%2Fsignin-oidc',
					},
				},
			}
		};

		if (window.msCommonShell) {
			window.msCommonShell.load(shellOptions);
		}
		else {
			window.onShellReadyToLoad = function () {
				window.onShellReadyToLoad = null;
				window.msCommonShell.load(shellOptions);
			};
		}
	});
</script>
